title: A Real CVE Triage Inside a 40k-Star Repository, Start to Finish
excerpt: We shadowed a maintainer through disclosure, patch review, and coordinated release for a dependency-chain vulnerability affecting thousands of downstream projects.

title: The Maintainer Burnout Problem Nobody's Roadmap Accounts For
excerpt: Interviews with five solo maintainers of widely-depended-on packages about triage fatigue, unpaid support labor, and what actually helped.

title: How Sponsor-Ware License Changes Are Redrawing the OSS Map
excerpt: More maintainers are experimenting with delayed-open licensing to fund full-time work. We tracked a dozen relicensing announcements to see what actually held up.

title: Why Three Major Frameworks Migrated Build Tools in the Same Quarter
excerpt: A shared upstream dependency change triggered a wave of build-tool swaps. We compare what each project chose and why the reasoning diverged.

title: Inside a Foundation Handoff: What Changes When a Project Stops Being One Person's
excerpt: A look at how a widely-used tooling project moved its trademark, CI budget, and release keys to a neutral foundation without losing its original maintainers.
